texto: In taking that photograph, I understood something I will never forget: how I wished to arrest all the beauty that came before me. Not the classical beauty of symmetry and exact proportions or the fancy of fashion, which is ever-changing with the seasons, but the beauty of a soul, that inner life that reveals itself so seldom, just for an instant, and only if you look closely and learn to see with an open heart.
explicacao: Citação de Elizabeth Ross sobre limites e escolhas conscientes. “In taking that photograph, I understood something I will never forget: how I wished to arrest all…” articula reflexao, beauty, heart em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como heart e people.
